title: Port-in delayed because account details do not match
summary: Resolve a mobile number transfer that is delayed because the losing carrier rejected the request due to account number, transfer PIN, or ZIP code mismatch.
symptoms: customer says transfer is stuck pending
port status tool shows mismatch or rejection
common_causes: incorrect transfer PIN or Number Transfer PIN
billing ZIP does not match the losing carrier record
old provider line is already disconnected
resolution_steps: Verify the account number exactly as shown on the prior carrier bill or app.
Regenerate the transfer PIN from the losing carrier if the existing PIN may have expired.
Confirm the billing ZIP code and account holder name are an exact match.
Resubmit the port request and monitor the status tool for a fresh response.
Keep the old line active until the port completes.
escalation_policy: Escalate to the porting team if the request remains rejected after the customer verifies all losing-carrier credentials.
related_articles: Phone shows SOS only after SIM swap
Short code and OTP texts are not arriving

title: Line suspended because of past-due balance
summary: Help a customer restore service after a non-pay suspension while clarifying minimum payment, reconnection timing, and partial payment rules.
resolution_steps: Review the account balance, failed payments, and the minimum amount required to restore service.
Take a payment or direct the customer to self-service billing.
Advise that reconnection can take up to 30 minutes after successful payment posting.
Have the customer restart the device once payment is reflected.
If autopay failed, update the payment method to prevent repeat suspensions.
escalation_policy: Escalate to billing support if the account remains suspended after payment posts and line status does not refresh.
related_articles: Voicemail PIN reset for mobile subscribers
Port-in delayed because account details do not match

title: Unexpected international roaming charges on the invoice
summary: Review roaming usage disputes and explain the difference between roaming passes, pay-per-use charges, and maritime or satellite usage exclusions.
symptoms: charges mention cruise ship or satellite network
common_causes: roaming pass was not active on all travel days
usage occurred on a non-covered maritime or airline network
device used background data before the pass started
secondary line on dual-SIM device generated the charges
resolution_steps: Review the invoice detail and identify the visited network and usage dates.
Check whether a roaming pass was active and when it started.
Explain any exclusions for maritime, satellite, or in-flight networks.
If the dispute appears valid, submit a billing review with the account notes and travel dates.
Recommend disabling background data or turning off the unused SIM before the next trip.
escalation_policy: Escalate to billing review when invoice details conflict with the active roaming pass coverage window.
related_articles: International roaming enabled but data will not connect
Line suspended because of past-due balance
